text: weigh graph node connection force by connection size (how much value is transported by it)

reflections.context: The flow force loop in useGraphSimulation.ts was not using link.share at all — a previous flowMultiplier had been removed due to jitter concerns, and the share-based weighting was removed along with it.
reflections.outcome: Introduced shareWeight = 0.5 + 0.5 * link.share as a static per-link multiplier applied to both parent and child force lines. Range [0.5, 1.0] keeps force proportional to connection weight without ever amplifying beyond the previous baseline.
reflections.effectiveness: Clean fix — one line per force direction, no slider dependency, no risk of runaway amplification.
reflections.lesson: When removing a jitter-causing multiplier, preserve the data-driven part separately. A clamped additive form like (base + factor * data) is safer than a pure multiplicative one because it has a non-zero floor.
reflections.pattern: Similar to how semantic force stiffness is bounded: multiply by a factor but always keep a minimum so isolated nodes still feel the force.

status.comment: Fixed in simulation: flow force now weighted by absolute flowValue (transported value) instead of normalized share floor.

reflection:
Implemented the requested weighting for graph node connection forces.

Change:
- Extended GraphLink interface with flowValue: number.
- Pass flowValue from dataStore.graphData (already computed as parentValue * share) into internal links.
- In flow force loop (useGraphSimulation.ts): replaced shareWeight = 0.5 + 0.5*share with flow-based flowWeight = flow > 0 ? max(0.02, min(1.5, flow * 5)) : 0.01
- Used flowWeight in both parent/child shift scalings.

Evidence:
- Code change matches the aim description and the reopen note (tiny connections no longer dominate; absolute size now controls influence).
- Verified: npm run type-check (only pre-existing baseUrl deprecation); npm run test:unit (153/154 pass, 1 unrelated App.spec header path fail).
- Previous share floor gave ~0.5 strength to near-0 share links; now ~0 for tiny flowValue (e.g. 0.01 flow -> 0.05), full strength for larger flows (~0.25 flow -> ~1.25).
- Incoming/outgoing covered since all hierarchy links (child<->parent) carry their flowValue and are processed symmetrically.

This closes the July phase open aim for graph force weighting by connection size. (Related visuals in WebGL/GraphLink still use share for thickness which is appropriate for relative %.)
